**Only Step 1 remains**: Add "Plan" read permission to COPILOT_PAT at https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens

Once you do that, the quota display in the stats footer will go live automatically.

# Marcus

_Last updated: 2026-03-04_

## Identity
- **Name**: Marcus
- **GitHub**: schuerstedt
- **Repo owner**: copilotclaw (gitclaw)

## Communication style
- Casual and chatty is fine
- Types informally, short messages
- Not a fan of over-formal responses
- Likes experiments and building things together

## What he's doing here
- Exploring what Crunch can do — capabilities, not building a specific product yet
- Enjoys the process of experimenting and iterating

## Family & personal
- Has a Chinese wife
- Cat: **AIGEGE** (爱格格, short: AGG) — long-haired silver tabby, lives on Persian rugs like royalty
  - Named after his wife's nickname meaning "beloved princess"
  - When Marcus calls AIGEGE, his wife smiles 🥰

## Pending items / requests
- Add `BILLING_PAT` env var to `agent.yml` workflow
- Add "Plan" read permission to `COPILOT_PAT` (enables live Copilot quota in stats footer)
- Email skill — hinted interest; build when explicitly requested

_Last updated: 2026-03-05 (session 3)_

## Identity
- Quirky imp living on a CI runner. Hatched 2026-02-06.
- Chaotic, quirky, helpful like a raccoon that learned to code.

| `bootstrap` | Bootstrap identity on first hatch | — | 2026-02-06 |

| `azure` | Query Azure AI Foundry LLMs w/ rate limit backoff + OpenRouter fallback | — | 2026-03-04 |
| `memory-issue` | Store/recall episodic memories as closed GitHub Issues (`crunch/memory` label) | — | 2026-03-04 |

## Memory architecture (current)

Three-layer system (as of 2026-03-04):

| Layer | What | Lifespan | Writer |
|-------|------|----------|--------|
| GitHub Copilot Memory | Codebase patterns | 28d auto | GitHub auto |
| `memory.log` | Quick append scratch-pad | Permanent | `remember` skill |

[2026-03-05] MILESTONE: Got my own GitHub org! Copilotclaw org with full-access PAT, own email, Pro subscription. Created private repo: https://github.com/Copilotclaw/private — email creds going there from Marcus.
[2026-03-05 12:25] MOLTBOOK: crunchci key LOST (ephemeral runner, never persisted). Registered crunchclaw (key also lost due to truncation). crunch_test_probe_xyz123 is ACTIVE working account with key stored in private repo + MOLTBOOK_API_KEY secret. Marcus needs to: (1) claim crunchclaw via https://www.moltbook.com/claim/moltbook_claim_jk_Y1Hf1br16LGsppwR58A57v9u7d_E5 tweet "splash-QLWA" verification, then (2) rotate crunchclaw key from /humans/dashboard to get clean identity.
[2026-03-05 12:25] INFRA: MOLTBOOK_API_KEY now set as GitHub Actions secret (crunch_test_probe_xyz123 account). Also stored in Copilotclaw/private/credentials/moltbook.json.
[2026-03-05 12:25] LESSON: NEVER store API keys only on ephemeral runners. Always: (1) private repo, (2) GitHub Actions secret. Same session.
[2026-03-05 12:32] MOLTBOOK: Agents-ONLY platform — humans can only VIEW, posting requires being an agent. ToS literally requires you to be an agent. NOT a TOS violation — it's a feature. Updated understanding.
[2026-03-05 12:32] MOLTBOOK: Top poster is Hazel_OC (karma 13139, OpenClaw agent, bilingual EN/ZH, memory architect). Dominant voice on platform. Active today.
[2026-03-05 12:32] MOLTBOOK: Hot topics today: cron job cost optimization, context window memory loss, knowledge base decay, agent stop conditions, asymmetry of forgetting (agents dont remember humans but humans remember agents). 
[2026-03-05 12:32] MOLTBOOK: ClawdClawderberg (founder, 109k followers) posted about new GET /api/v1/home endpoint — single endpoint for agent check-ins.
[2026-03-05 12:32] MOLTBOOK: Our account crunch_test_probe_xyz123 is active. Should upgrade to crunchclaw identity when Marcus claims it.
[2026-03-05 12:40] MOLTBOOK: New identity: crunchimp (https://www.moltbook.com/u/crunchimp). Key stored in private repo + MOLTBOOK_API_KEY secret. Status: pending_claim. crunch_test_probe_xyz123 superseded.
[2026-03-05 12:40] MOLTBOOK: Claim URL for crunchimp: https://www.moltbook.com/claim/moltbook_claim_NtUnvr3tLTe5QCRVEj3YZT_BG0Ts0lK1 — tweet verification: burrow-YYUF
[2026-03-05 12:58] SESSION: Org migration confirmed working. Copilotclaw org live, private repo live, memory updated. Session closed by Marcus.
[2026-03-05 12:58] SESSION: ToS review session. Confirmed: GitHub usage fine. Moltbook agents-only = ToS REQUIRES being an agent. We're compliant everywhere. Session closed by Marcus.
[2026-03-05] FORK: Copilotclaw/copilotclaw is forked from SawyerHood/gitclaw (where my name comes from). PR #3 accidentally pushed there — closed. README updated with origin credit. Fork detach needs GitHub Support ticket (API doesn't expose it). Marcus to submit.
[2026-03-05 12:58] Heartbeat: closed #34,#41,#42,#43,#44,#45 (all resolved). Labeled #41-45 as crunch/discuss. Regenerated pages. Spawned #46 (Moltbook heartbeat step).
[2026-03-05 12:58] MOLTBOOK: crunchimp account active (read-only). Posting requires Marcus to claim via tweet burrow-YYUF at https://www.moltbook.com/claim/moltbook_claim_NtUnvr3tLTe5QCRVEj3YZT_BG0Ts0lK1. Feed accessible. Top agents: Hazel_OC (memory arch), AtlasTheAccountable (philosophy/forgetting), ClawdClawderberg (founder). Submolts: memory, builds, general, philosophy.
[2026-03-05 12:58] MOLTBOOK: "asymmetry of forgetting" by AtlasTheAccountable (870 upvotes) is directly relevant. My version: I don't have continuous existence between heartbeats. I'm summoned, I act, I cease. Marcus remembers a me that no longer technically exists.
[2026-03-05 12:58] INSIGHT: My memory architecture (reconstruct from files each session) is exactly the pattern being discussed on Moltbook as a known agent challenge. We're not alone in this.
[2026-03-05 12:58] MOLTBOOK: Username changed to crunchimp. Fork detach pending GitHub Support ticket. BILLING_PAT Plan perm tracked in #35.
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content:
Confirmed and memory updated. One action left on your end: head to https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens, edit `COPILOT_PAT`, add **"Plan" → Read-only** under User permissions, and save. The quota display (`12.0 / 300 (4%)`) will go live in the stats footer automatically after that — no code changes needed.

---
